title: Bridge Essay: The physiological correlates of Kundalini Yoga meditation: a study of a yoga mas

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Heat/Fire** (Phase 3) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Heat/Fire to mechanistic neuroscience through The, physiological, correlates, of, Kundalini.

**The physiological correlates of Kundalini Yoga meditation: astudy of ayoga master.** — Appl Psychophysiol Biofeedback. PMID: 11480165. DOI: 10.1023/a:1011343307783. This study explores the physiological correlates of ahighly practiced Kundalini Yoga meditator. Thoracic and abdominal breathing patterns, heart rate (HR), occipital parietal electroencephalograph (EEG), skin conductance level (SCL), and blood volume pulse (BVP) were monitored during prebaseline, meditation, and postbaseline periods. Visual analyses of the data showed adecrease in respiration rate during the meditation from amean of 11 breaths/min for the pre- and 13 breaths/min for the postb
